title: ephedra distachya subsp. distachya
intro: Ephedra distachya subsp. distachya, also known as Joint Pine or Joint Fir, is a plant species in the Ephedraceae family, native to Asia and Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a natural stimulant, decongestant, and anti-inflammatory agent, thanks to the presence of ephedrine, a key alkaloid. The health benefits of Ephedra distachya subsp. distachya include relief from colds, flu, and asthma, as well as improved circulation and weight loss. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and stems are often dried and used as a tea or added to supplements, although its use is heavily regulated in many countries due to concerns over side effects and interactions with other medications.
